---
title: "Experience"
format: html
css: styles.css
---

This page provides an overview of my professional journey and how my work has progressively evolved around data, from experiments to increasingly data-driven, applied and collaborative projects.

Over the years, I have worked on a wide range of topics in epidemiology, vector biology, public health, and intervention evaluation, with a common thread: using data analysis to better understand complex systems and support informed decisions.

Through these experiences, I have developed strong skills in statistical modelling, study design, data management and the analysis of longitudinal and time-dependent data, often in interdisciplinary and international environments.

My more recent projects have involved growing collaboration with external and industrial partners, where analytical results contribute directly to project orientation, innovation and strategic choices.

::: {.timeline}

::: {.timeline-item}
**01/2024–12/2025**  

#### Biostatistician (Researcher) — IRD  
Montpellier  

*Biostatistics – Behavioural data analysis and machine learning*

- Designed studies and coordinated project activities with multidisciplinary team members  
- Managed and structured complex behavioural datasets derived from video recordings, including curation and storage  
- Developed data processing workflows for feature extraction and behavioural metrics generation  
- Performed statistical analyses including multivariate methods and data visualization  
- Applied machine learning approaches (SVM) and Hidden Markov Models (HMM) to classify and characterize larvae movement patterns  
- Built reproducible analysis pipelines in R for data processing and reporting  
- Contributed to scientific writing and dissemination of results  

**Impact:**  
- Generated new insights into the sublethal effects of insecticides on mosquito larvae behaviour and life-history traits  
- Improved the quantitative characterization of behavioural responses using data-driven approaches  

🔗 [Project: Mosquito larvae movement](projects/project_movLarv.qmd)
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
**05/2021–12/2023**  
 
#### Biostatistician (Researcher) — IRD  
Montpellier  

*Biostatistics – Pre-clinical efficacy assessment*

- Designed studies and developed Statistical Analysis Plans (SAP), including sample size estimation  
- Managed and curated complex experimental datasets, ensuring data quality and consistency  
- Performed data analysis using survival models, GLMM, PK/PD modelling and epidemiological modelling (SEIR)  
- Developed data visualizations and statistical reports for diverse stakeholders  
- Translated analytical results into actionable insights to support project decisions and go/no-go strategies  
- Collaborated with multidisciplinary and international teams, including industrial partners  
- Communicated results through consortium meetings and international conferences  
- Supervised students and trained collaborators in statistical analysis and data handling  

**Impact:**  
- Contributed to the identification of optimal dose and long-acting formulation  
- Supported strategies aimed at reducing malaria transmission through data-driven decision-making  

🔗 [Project: IMPACT](projects/project_IMPACT.qmd)  
🔗 [Project: Anivermate](projects/project_anivermate.qmd)  
🔗 [Project: RADO](projects/groupe_biostat.qmd)

:::

::: {.timeline-item}
**06/2020–12/2020**  

#### Biostatistician (Researcher) — IRD 

Montpellier

*Biostatistics - Vector ecology*

- Data analysis : rhythm parameters estimation (chisq and FFT periodogramm, onset time, time of maximum activity), GLMM with zero inflation, Hidden Markov Model (HMM)
- Data management and visualisation
- Data driven analysis 
- Results : scientific paper co-writing.  
- Supervision of students (Msc and PhD students)
- Impact: This project advanced our understanding of how *Anopheles coluzzii* activity rhythms respond to both external and internal variability.

🔗 [Project: Anorythm](projects/project_anorythm.qmd)
:::

::: {.timeline-item}
**09/2018–08/2019**  

#### Msc Biostatistics — Montpellier University 

Montpellier

*Biostatistics - Data analysis - Machine Learning - R*

- Programming : R - SAS - SQL 
- Data management : SQL databases
- Omics : ML, high dimension reduction, variable selection, genetic algorithm
- Spatial and temporal statistics 
- GLMM, censored data, multi-states models 

Internship : [Project: Anorythm](projects/project_anorythm.qmd)
:::

::: {.timeline-item}

**04/2017–02/2020**  

#### Engeneer - IRD

Montpellier

*Biostatistics - Experimentations - Insecticides and repellent*

- Data analysis : R, GLMM, survival analysis (KM and mixed cox model)
- Experiments : Bioassay test with insecticides (OMS), behavioral experiment with mosquitoes, 
electrophysiology (EAG and SSR). GC/MS use. 
::: 

::: {.timeline-item}

**11/2016–12/2016**  

#### Technician - IRD 

Montpellier

*Mosquitoes mass rearing*
::: 

::: {.timeline-item}
**10/2012–12/2015**  

#### PhD - IRD {.job-title}

Cotonou/Montpellier

*Behavioural ecology*

**Behaviour and olfaction of *Anopheles gambiae* : Interaction entre mecanisme de résistance et insecticide.**

- Statistics : R, GLMM, survival analysis (KM and mixed cox model)
- Experiments : Olfactometry, behavioral experiment with mosquitoes, 
electrophysiology (EAG and SSR). GC/MS use. 

<i class="bi bi-book-half"></i> [Thesis](content/pdf/Manuscript_VF1 -diffusion.pdf)
::: 

::: {.timeline-item}
**09/2010–08/2012**  

#### MsC degree in Epidemiology - University of Montpellier

La Reunion Island/Montpellier

*Animal Health and Epidemiology in Southern countries*

- Epidemiology
- Statistics : R, GLMM, survival analysis (KM and mixed cox model)
- Internship : Risk assessment of the introduction of *Varroa destructor* in La Réunion Island. 
Creation of database of beekeepers of the Island, survey redaction and application about beekeeping practices. Reporting to expert and beekeepers. 
::: 

:::
